Benchmark Note: No direct safe counterpart; compiler-assumption intrinsic-style API.
Safety Condition: Reaching this function is *Undefined Behavior*. As the compiler assumes that all forms of Undefined Behavior can never happen, it will eliminate all branches in the surrounding code that it can determine will invariably lead to a call to `unreachable_unchecked()`. If the assumptions embedded in using this function turn out to be wrong - that is, if the site which is calling `unreachable_unchecked()` is actually reachable at runtime - the compiler may have generated nonsensical machine instructions for this situation, including in seemingly unrelated code, causing difficult-to-debug problems. Use this function sparingly. Consider using the [`unreachable!`] macro, which may prevent some optimizations but will safely panic in case it is actually reached at runtime. Benchmark your code to find out if using `unreachable_unchecked()` comes with a performance benefit.
